records.question: Which bookings were made by customers who became customers exactly on 2016-02-03 16:58:11 and that are returned damaged?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT bookings.booking_id FROM bookings
JOIN customers ON bookings.customer_id = customers.customer_id
WHERE customers.date_became_customer = '2016-02-03 16:58:11' AND bookings.returned_damaged_yn = '1'
records.pred_sql: select bookings.booking_id from bookings join customers on bookings.customer_id = customers.customer_id where customers.date_became_customer = '2016-02-03 16:58:11' and bookings.returned_damaged_yn = 'y' limit 5;
records.thought: We need bookings where the associated customer became a customer on '2016-02-03 16:58:11' and the booking was returned damaged. This requires joining Bookings with Customers and filtering on both conditions.

records.question: Which bookings have an amount of discount of at least 39.27 and an amount outstanding exactly equal to 56.02?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT bookings.booking_id FROM bookings
JOIN customers ON bookings.customer_id = customers.customer_id
JOIN discount_coupons ON customers.coupon_id = discount_coupons.coupon_id
WHERE bookings.amount_of_discount >= 39.27 AND bookings.amount_outstanding = 56.02
records.pred_sql: select booking_id from bookings where amount_of_discount >= 39.27 and amount_outstanding = 56.02 limit 5;
records.thought: We need to filter the Bookings table for records where amount_of_discount is at least 39.27 and amount_outstanding is exactly 56.02, and select booking_id with a limit of 5.

records.question: Which bookings have an outstanding amount of 64.01, include products booked for exactly 102.76 that were not returned late, come from customers hired on or after 2018-03-04 08:59:40, and were returned damaged?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT bookings.booking_id FROM bookings
JOIN customers ON bookings.customer_id = customers.customer_id
JOIN products_booked ON bookings.booking_id = products_booked.booking_id
WHERE products_booked.booked_amount = 102.76 AND customers.date_last_hire >= '2018-03-04 08:59:40' AND products_booked.returned_late_yn = '0' AND bookings.returned_damaged_yn = '1' AND bookings.amount_outstanding = 64.01
records.pred_sql: select bookings.booking_id from bookings join products_booked on bookings.booking_id = products_booked.booking_id join customers on bookings.customer_id = customers.customer_id where bookings.amount_outstanding = 64.01 and products_booked.booked_amount = 102.76 and products_booked.returned_late_yn = 'N' and customers.date_became_customer >= '2018-03-04 08:59:40' and bookings.returned_damaged_yn = 'Y' limit 5;
records.thought: We need bookings with amount_outstanding = 64.01, joined to products_booked with booked_amount = 102.76 and returned_late_yn = 'N', joined to customers with date_became_customer >= '2018-03-04 08:59:40', and bookings.returned_damaged_yn = 'Y'.

records.question: Which bookings have a refund amount at most 143.99, where count_hired is 499, made by female customers with the last name Crona who became customers exactly on 2017-07-27 08:04:22?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT bookings.booking_id FROM bookings
JOIN customers ON bookings.customer_id = customers.customer_id
WHERE customers.date_became_customer = '2017-07-27 08:04:22' AND bookings.amount_of_refund <= 143.99 AND bookings.count_hired = '499' AND customers.gender_mf = '0' AND customers.last_name = 'Crona'
records.pred_sql: select bookings.booking_id from bookings join customers on bookings.customer_id = customers.customer_id where bookings.amount_of_refund <= 143.99 and bookings.count_hired = 499 and lower(customers.gender_mf) = 'f' and lower(customers.last_name) like '%crona%' and customers.date_became_customer = '2017-07-27 08:04:22' limit 5;
records.thought: We need to filter bookings by amount_of_refund <= 143.99, count_hired = 499, and join with customers to filter by gender_mf = 'f', last_name like '%crona%', and date_became_customer = '2017-07-27 08:04:22'.

records.question: How many bookings started on or after 2017-05-24 16:16:29 and ended on or after 2018-03-07 17:39:04, with no damage returned, an amount payable of at most 374.59, and an amount of refund at most 130.87, made by male customers named Terry who became customers on or before 2015-05-05 20:29:01?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT bookings.booking_id FROM bookings
JOIN customers ON bookings.customer_id = customers.customer_id
WHERE bookings.amount_of_refund <= 130.87 AND customers.date_became_customer <= '2015-05-05 20:29:01' AND customers.gender_mf = '1' AND bookings.booking_start_date >= '2017-05-24 16:16:29' AND bookings.amount_payable <= 374.59 AND customers.last_name = 'Terry' AND bookings.returned_damaged_yn = '0' AND bookings.booking_end_date >= '2018-03-07 17:39:04'
records.pred_sql: select bookings.booking_id from bookings join customers on bookings.customer_id = customers.customer_id where bookings.booking_start_date >= '2017-05-24 16:16:29' and bookings.booking_end_date >= '2018-03-07 17:39:04' and bookings.returned_damaged_yn = 'N' and bookings.amount_payable <= 374.59 and bookings.amount_of_refund <= 130.87 and customers.gender_mf = 'M' and lower(customers.first_name) like '%terry%' and customers.date_became_customer <= '2015-05-05 20:29:01' limit 5;
records.thought: We need to join Bookings with Customers to filter by male customers named Terry who became customers on or before the specified date. Then, we apply all the date, damage, amount, and refund filters as described.

records.question: Which bookings have an amount of refund at most 161.9 and have related customers and products booked?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT bookings.booking_id FROM bookings
JOIN customers ON bookings.customer_id = customers.customer_id
JOIN products_booked ON bookings.booking_id = products_booked.booking_id
WHERE bookings.amount_of_refund <= 161.9
records.pred_sql: select bookings.booking_id from bookings join customers on bookings.customer_id = customers.customer_id join products_booked on bookings.booking_id = products_booked.booking_id where bookings.amount_of_refund <= 161.9 limit 5;
records.thought: We need bookings where amount_of_refund <= 161.9, and ensure there are related customers and products booked by joining with Customers and Products_Booked.

records.question: Which bookings have a start date on or before 2017-05-24 16:16:29?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT bookings.booking_id FROM bookings
WHERE bookings.booking_start_date <= '2017-05-24 16:16:29'
records.pred_sql: select bookings.booking_id from bookings where booking_start_date <= '2017-05-24 16:16:29' limit 5;
records.thought: We need to filter bookings where the booking_start_date is on or before the specified date and select booking_id with a limit of 5.

records.question: Which bookings that are returned damaged have an amount of discount at least 49.26, start on 2017-04-04 11:02:43, end on 2018-02-01 09:30:50, where count hired is 729, amount outstanding is 66.01, and amount of refund is at least 135.94?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT bookings.booking_id FROM bookings
WHERE bookings.returned_damaged_yn = '1' AND bookings.amount_of_discount >= 49.26 AND bookings.booking_start_date = '2017-04-04 11:02:43' AND bookings.booking_end_date = '2018-02-01 09:30:50' AND bookings.count_hired = '729' AND bookings.amount_outstanding = 66.01 AND bookings.amount_of_refund >= 135.94
records.pred_sql: select bookings.booking_id from bookings where returned_damaged_yn = 1 and amount_of_discount >= 49.26 and booking_start_date = '2017-04-04 11:02:43' and booking_end_date = '2018-02-01 09:30:50' and count_hired = 729 and amount_outstanding = 66.01 and amount_of_refund >= 135.94 limit 5;
records.thought: We need to filter bookings where returned_damaged_yn is true, amount_of_discount is at least 49.26, booking_start_date and booking_end_date match the given values, count_hired is 729, amount_outstanding is 66.01, and amount_of_refund is at least 135.94.

records.question: What bookings with a booking status code of Provisional have an amount outstanding exactly 36.79 and a booking end date on or before 2018-03-04 21:42:45?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT bookings.booking_id FROM bookings
WHERE bookings.amount_outstanding = 36.79 AND bookings.booking_status_code = 'Provisional' AND bookings.booking_end_date <= '2018-03-04 21:42:45'
records.pred_sql: select bookings.booking_id from bookings where bookings.booking_status_code like '%provisional%' and bookings.amount_outstanding = 36.79 and bookings.booking_end_date <= '2018-03-04 21:42:45' limit 5;
records.thought: We need to filter bookings where booking_status_code is 'Provisional', amount_outstanding is exactly 36.79, and booking_end_date is on or before '2018-03-04 21:42:45'.

records.question: Which confirmed bookings that ended on or after 2018-01-01 10:19:20 have exactly 843 hired items, a discount of at most 24.19, come from good male customers who joined on or before 2017-07-27 08:04:22, and include products that were returned?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT bookings.booking_id FROM bookings
JOIN customers ON bookings.customer_id = customers.customer_id
JOIN products_booked ON bookings.booking_id = products_booked.booking_id
WHERE bookings.booking_status_code = 'Confirmed' AND bookings.booking_end_date >= '2018-01-01 10:19:20' AND customers.date_became_customer <= '2017-07-27 08:04:22' AND bookings.count_hired = '843' AND customers.good_or_bad_customer = 'good' AND products_booked.returned_yn = '1' AND bookings.amount_of_discount <= 24.19 AND customers.gender_mf = '0'
records.pred_sql:
select bookings.booking_id
from bookings
join customers on bookings.customer_id = customers.customer_id
join products_booked on bookings.booking_id = products_booked.booking_id
where bookings.booking_status_code like '%confirmed%'
  and bookings.booking_end_date >= '2018-01-01 10:19:20'
  and bookings.count_hired = 843
  and bookings.amount_of_discount <= 24.19
  and customers.good_or_bad_customer like '%good%'
  and customers.gender_mf like '%m%'
  and customers.date_became_customer <= '2017-07-27 08:04:22'
  and products_booked.returned_yn = 'y'
limit 5;
records.thought: We need to join Bookings, Customers, and Products_Booked to filter by booking status, end date, count_hired, discount, customer attributes, and ensure at least one product was returned. We'll use appropriate where conditions and limit 5.
